answer:
LinkedIn Ads APIs utilize OAuth 2.0 for authentication [1][2]. To authorize requests, you must implement the 3-legged (3L) OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow to obtain a member access token [3][4]. 

Your application must be configured in the LinkedIn Developer Portal with the appropriate permission scopes assigned, such as r_ads for read-only access or rw_ads for read/write operations [1][2][5]. When making API calls, include the access token in the Authorization header as a Bearer token [6]. Additionally, you must provide a Linkedin-Version header to specify the API version [6].

```
curl -X GET 'https://api.linkedin.com/rest/adAccountUsers?q=authenticatedUser' \
-H 'Authorization: Bearer {YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN}' \
-H 'Linkedin-Version: 202601'
```
